revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue ware pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category|Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} *[http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?page_id=592 Toyohashi Museum of Art and History home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyohashi]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue ware pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue ware pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue ware pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue ware pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue ware pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue ware]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue ware pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} *[http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?page_id=592 Toyohashi Museum of Art and History home page] {{in lang|ja}}

These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} *[http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?page_id=592 Toyohashi Museum of Art and History home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} *[http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?page_id=592 Toyohashi Museum of Art and History home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} *[http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?page_id=592 Toyohashi Museum of Art and History home page] {{in lang|ja}}

==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} *[http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?page_id=592 Toyohashi Museum of Art and History home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} *[http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?page_id=592 Toyohashi Museum of Art and History home page] {{in lang|ja}}
