These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|48|54|N|137|26|52|E|region:JP-22|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Toyohashi, Aichi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} *[http://www.toyohashi-bihaku.jp/?page_id=592 Toyohashi Museum of Art and History home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24874799]] Wikidata name: Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.815|N|137.4477777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishimakihon-machi|qid=Q118398495}} | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q24874799]] Wikidata name: Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.815|N|137.4477777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishimakihon-machi|qid=Q118398495}} | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun]][[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]][[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24874799]] Wikidata name: Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.815|N|137.4477777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishimakihon-machi|WD=Q118398495}} | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun]][[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]][[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24874799]] Wikidata name: Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.815|N|137.4477777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishimakihon-machi|WD=Q118398495}} | region = [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].

These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], which is the purported tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including a gold-plated [[:en:Horse harness|Horse harness]]. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]].
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.

The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun]][[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]][[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun]][[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]][[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun]][[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]][[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q24874799]] Wikidata name: Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.815|N|137.4477777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishimakihon-machi|WD=Q118398495}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara}}, which is the purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}.
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including a gold-plated {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato state}} to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}}.
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|WD=Q9652090|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)}}

cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara}}, which is the purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}.
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including a gold-plated {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato state}} to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}}.
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

The chamber was found to contain a large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including a gold-plated {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato state}} to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}}.
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.815|N|137.4477777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishimakihon-machi|WD=Q118398495}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara}}, which is the purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}.
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including a gold-plated {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato state}} to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}}.
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>

[[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Magoshinagahizuka Kofun}} [[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q24874799}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun]][[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]][[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun]][[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]][[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Magoshinagahizuka Kofun]][[en:Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun]][[ja:馬越長火塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.815|N|137.4477777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishimakihon-machi|WD=Q118398495|1=Ishimakihon-machi}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, which is the purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}.
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including a gold-plated {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato state|1=Yamato Kingship}} to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}.
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|WD=Q9652090|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | native_name =馬越長火塚古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Magoshinagahizuka Tomb.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Aichi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.815|N|137.4477777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishimakihon-machi|WD=Q118398495|1=Ishimakihon-machi}} | region = {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = None | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun '''|馬越長火塚古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ishimakihonmachi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Toyohashi, Aichi|Toyohashi, Aichi]] in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, which is the purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}.
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including a gold-plated {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato state|1=Yamato Kingship}} to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}.
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
<gallery> File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 1.jpg|One of the harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 2.jpg|7 harness pendants File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 3.jpg|Harness fittings, etc. File:Artifacts excavated from the Magoshinagahizuka Kofun 4.jpg|Sue pottery pottery </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|WD=Q9652090|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Aichi)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|qid=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, which is the purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}.
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including a gold-plated {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|qid=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2016.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/245821|title=馬越長火塚古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Magoshi-Nagahizuka Kofun is one of the largest ''kofun'' in the Tōkai region. It has a length of 70 meters and height of 5.5 meters, and is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period, at around the end of the 6th century AD.
The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpō-kōen-fun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. However, this tumulus is unusual in that there is a large disparity between the posterior circular portion and the anterior rectangular portion. The closest other example of this design is the Misemaruyama Kofun in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|qid=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, which is the purported tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}.
The tomb contains a multi-chambered stone-lined passageway with a length of 17 meters, and a multi-room stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].The ceiling has an arc-shaped vertical cross section and there is a single megalith forming the back wall. The chamber was found to contain a large number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including a gold-plated {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|qid=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}. This type of harness, with apricot-leaf shaped decorations, is known from other burials to have been given by the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato state|1=Yamato Kingship}} to the highest-Ranking local lords under its suzerainty, and therefore this tomb is likely to be that of the local king of East Mikawa.
In 1944, during the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the burial chamber was used as an ammunition storehouse by the {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|qid=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army|1=Imperial Japanese Army}}.
Some 311 items which were excavated from the ''kofun'' were collectively designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2012. These included an extensive number of gold-plated horse fittings, including bells, bridles, harnesses, and stirrups, and well as sword, spears, bows and arrows, agricultural implements, and a large number of beads made from various materials and in various sizes and shapes, as well as a large number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/263726|title=愛知県馬越長火塚古墳出土品|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
The site is located on a narrow farm road surrounded by persimmon fields some distance from the prefectural road. There is a parking lot for visitors, but no other public facilities.
